143	class EulerDiscreteScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
144	    """
145	    Euler scheduler.
146	
147	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
148	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
149	
150	    Args:
151	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to 1000):
152	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
153	        beta_start (`float`, defaults to 0.0001):
154	            The starting `beta` value of inference.
155	        beta_end (`float`, defaults to 0.02):
156	            The final `beta` value.
157	        beta_schedule (`Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]`, defaults to `"linear"`):
158	            The beta schedule, a mapping from a beta range to a sequence of betas for stepping the model. Choose from
159	            `"linear"`, `"scaled_linear"`, or `"squaredcos_cap_v2"`.
160	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):
161	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
162	        prediction_type (`Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]`, defaults to `"epsilon"`, *optional*):
163	            Prediction type of the scheduler function; can be `"epsilon"` (predicts the noise of the diffusion
164	            process), `"sample"` (directly predicts the noisy sample`) or `"v_prediction"` (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
165	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper).
166	        interpolation_type (`Literal["linear", "log_linear"]`, defaults to `"linear"`, *optional*):
167	            The interpolation type to compute intermediate sigmas for the scheduler denoising steps. Should be one of
168	            `"linear"` or `"log_linear"`.
169	        use_karras_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
170	            Whether to use Karras sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `True`,
171	            the sigmas are determined according to a sequence of noise levels {σi}.
172	        use_exponential_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
173	            Whether to use exponential sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process.
174	        use_beta_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
175	            Whether to use beta sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. Refer to [Beta
176	            Sampling is All You Need](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.12173) for more information.
177	        sigma_min (`float`, *optional*):
178	            The minimum sigma value for the noise schedule. If not provided, defaults to the last sigma in the
179	            schedule.
180	        sigma_max (`float`, *optional*):
181	            The maximum sigma value for the noise schedule. If not provided, defaults to the first sigma in the
182	            schedule.
183	        timestep_spacing (`Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"]`, defaults to `"linspace"`):
184	            The way the timesteps should be scaled. Refer to Table 2 of the [Common Diffusion Noise Schedules and
185	            Sample Steps are Flawed](https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891) for more information.
186	        timestep_type (`Literal["discrete", "continuous"]`, defaults to `"discrete"`):
187	            The type of timesteps to use. Can be `"discrete"` or `"continuous"`.
188	        steps_offset (`int`, defaults to 0):
189	            An offset added to the inference steps, as required by some model families.
190	        rescale_betas_zero_snr (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
191	            Whether to rescale the betas to have zero terminal SNR. This enables the model to generate very bright and
192	            dark samples instead of limiting it to samples with medium brightness. Loosely related to
193	            [`--offset_noise`](https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/blob/74fd735eb073eb1d774b1ab4154a0876eb82f055/examples/dreambooth/train_dreambooth.py#L506).
194	        final_sigmas_type (`Literal["zero", "sigma_min"]`, defaults to `"zero"`):
195	            The final `sigma` value for the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `"sigma_min"`, the final
196	            sigma is the same as the last sigma in the training schedule. If `"zero"`, the final sigma is set to 0.
197	    """
198	
199	    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
200	    order = 1
201	
202	    @register_to_config
203	    def __init__(
204	        self,
205	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
206	        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
207	        beta_end: float = 0.02,
208	        beta_schedule: str = "linear",
209	        trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
210	        prediction_type: str = "epsilon",
211	        interpolation_type: str = "linear",
212	        use_karras_sigmas: bool | None = False,
213	        use_exponential_sigmas: bool | None = False,
214	        use_beta_sigmas: bool | None = False,
215	        sigma_min: float | None = None,
216	        sigma_max: float | None = None,
217	        timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "linspace",
218	        timestep_type: Literal["discrete", "continuous"] = "discrete",
219	        steps_offset: int = 0,
220	        rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
221	        final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
222	    ):
223	        if self.config.use_beta_sigmas and not is_scipy_available():
224	            raise ImportError("Make sure to install scipy if you want to use beta sigmas.")
225	        if sum([self.config.use_beta_sigmas, self.config.use_exponential_sigmas, self.config.use_karras_sigmas]) > 1:
226	            raise ValueError(
227	                "Only one of `config.use_beta_sigmas`, `config.use_exponential_sigmas`, `config.use_karras_sigmas` can be used."
228	            )
229	        if trained_betas is not None:
230	            self.betas = torch.tensor(trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
231	        elif beta_schedule == "linear":
232	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)
233	        elif beta_schedule == "scaled_linear":
234	            # this schedule is very specific to the latent diffusion model.
235	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start**0.5, beta_end**0.5, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32) ** 2
236	        elif beta_schedule == "squaredcos_cap_v2":
237	            # Glide cosine schedule
238	            self.betas = betas_for_alpha_bar(num_train_timesteps)
239	        else:
240	            raise NotImplementedError(f"{beta_schedule} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")
241	
242	        if rescale_betas_zero_snr:
243	            self.betas = rescale_zero_terminal_snr(self.betas)
244	
245	        self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
246	        self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)
247	
248	        if rescale_betas_zero_snr:
249	            # Close to 0 without being 0 so first sigma is not inf
250	            # FP16 smallest positive subnormal works well here
251	            self.alphas_cumprod[-1] = 2**-24
252	
253	        sigmas = (((1 - self.alphas_cumprod) / self.alphas_cumprod) ** 0.5).flip(0)
254	        timesteps = np.linspace(0, num_train_timesteps - 1, num_train_timesteps, dtype=float)[::-1].copy()
255	        timesteps = torch.from_numpy(timesteps).to(dtype=torch.float32)
256	
257	        # setable values
258	        self.num_inference_steps = None
259	
260	        # TODO: Support the full EDM scalings for all prediction types and timestep types
261	        if timestep_type == "continuous" and prediction_type == "v_prediction":
262	            self.timesteps = torch.Tensor([0.25 * sigma.log() for sigma in sigmas])
263	        else:
264	            self.timesteps = timesteps
265	
266	        self.sigmas = torch.cat([sigmas, torch.zeros(1, device=sigmas.device)])
267	
268	        self.is_scale_input_called = False
269	        self.use_karras_sigmas = use_karras_sigmas
270	        self.use_exponential_sigmas = use_exponential_sigmas
271	        self.use_beta_sigmas = use_beta_sigmas
272	
273	        self._step_index = None
274	        self._begin_index = None
275	        self.sigmas = self.sigmas.to("cpu")  # to avoid too much CPU/GPU communication
276	
277	    @property
278	    def init_noise_sigma(self) -> float | torch.Tensor:
279	        """
280	        The standard deviation of the initial noise distribution.
281	
282	        Returns:
283	            `float` or `torch.Tensor`:
284	                The standard deviation of the initial noise distribution, computed based on the maximum sigma value and
285	                the timestep spacing configuration.
286	        """
287	        max_sigma = max(self.sigmas) if isinstance(self.sigmas, list) else self.sigmas.max()
288	        if self.config.timestep_spacing in ["linspace", "trailing"]:
289	            return max_sigma
290	
291	        return (max_sigma**2 + 1) ** 0.5
292	
293	    @property
294	    def step_index(self) -> int:
295	        """
296	        The index counter for current timestep. It will increase by 1 after each scheduler step.
297	
298	        Returns:
299	            `int` or `None`:
300	                The current step index, or `None` if not initialized.

13	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):

28	    def test_betas(self):

33	        for schedule in ["linear", "scaled_linear"]:

39

42	        for timestep_type in timestep_types:

50	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
51	
52	    def full_loop(self, **config):
53	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
54	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
55	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
56	
57	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
58	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
59	
60	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
61	
62	        model = self.dummy_model()
63	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
64	        sample = sample.to(torch_device)
65	
66	        for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
67	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
68	
69	            model_output = model(sample, t)
70	
71	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
72	            sample = output.prev_sample
73	        return sample

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738

743	    @functools.wraps(original_init)
744	    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
745	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
746	            raise RuntimeError(
747	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
748	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
749	            )
750	
751	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init. Retrieve all passed attributes
752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())
753	
754	        # Retrieve default values

756	        default_kwargs = {}
757	        for field in fields:
758	            # ignore flax specific attributes
759	            if field.name in self._flax_internal_args:
760	                continue
761	            if type(field.default) == dataclasses._MISSING_TYPE:
762	                default_kwargs[field.name] = None
763	            else:
764	                default_kwargs[field.name] = getattr(self, field.name)
765	
766	        # Make sure init_kwargs override default kwargs
767	        new_kwargs = {**default_kwargs, **init_kwargs}

770	            new_kwargs.pop("dtype")
771	
772	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
773	        for i, arg in enumerate(args):
774	            name = fields[i].name
775	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
776	
777	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
778	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
779	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
780	
781	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
782	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)

784	    cls.__init__ = init
785	    return cls

288	def get_scheduler(
289	    name: str | SchedulerType,
290	    optimizer: Optimizer,
291	    step_rules: str | None = None,
292	    num_warmup_steps: int | None = None,
293	    num_training_steps: int | None = None,
294	    num_cycles: int = 1,
295	    power: float = 1.0,
296	    last_epoch: int = -1,
297	) -> LambdaLR:
298	    """
299	    Unified API to get any scheduler from its name.
300	
301	    Args:
302	        name (`str` or `SchedulerType`):
303	            The name of the scheduler to use.
304	        optimizer (`torch.optim.Optimizer`):
305	            The optimizer that will be used during training.
306	        step_rules (`str`, *optional*):
307	            A string representing the step rules to use. This is only used by the `PIECEWISE_CONSTANT` scheduler.
308	        num_warmup_steps (`int`, *optional*):
309	            The number of warmup steps to do. This is not required by all schedulers (hence the argument being
310	            optional), the function will raise an error if it's unset and the scheduler type requires it.
311	        num_training_steps (`int``, *optional*):
312	            The number of training steps to do. This is not required by all schedulers (hence the argument being
313	            optional), the function will raise an error if it's unset and the scheduler type requires it.
314	        num_cycles (`int`, *optional*):
315	            The number of hard restarts used in `COSINE_WITH_RESTARTS` scheduler.
316	        power (`float`, *optional*, defaults to 1.0):
317	            Power factor. See `POLYNOMIAL` scheduler
318	        last_epoch (`int`, *optional*, defaults to -1):
319	            The index of the last epoch when resuming training.
320	    """
321	    name = SchedulerType(name)
322	    schedule_func = TYPE_TO_SCHEDULER_FUNCTION[name]
323	    if name == SchedulerType.CONSTANT:
324	        return schedule_func(optimizer, last_epoch=last_epoch)
325	
326	    if name == SchedulerType.PIECEWISE_CONSTANT:
327	        return schedule_func(optimizer, step_rules=step_rules, last_epoch=last_epoch)
328	
329	    # All other schedulers require `num_warmup_steps`
330	    if num_warmup_steps is None:
331	        raise ValueError(f"{name} requires `num_warmup_steps`, please provide that argument.")
332	
333	    if name == SchedulerType.CONSTANT_WITH_WARMUP:
334	        return schedule_func(optimizer, num_warmup_steps=num_warmup_steps, last_epoch=last_epoch)
335	
336	    # All other schedulers require `num_training_steps`
337	    if num_training_steps is None:
338	        raise ValueError(f"{name} requires `num_training_steps`, please provide that argument.")
339	
340	    if name == SchedulerType.COSINE_WITH_RESTARTS:
341	        return schedule_func(
342	            optimizer,
343	            num_warmup_steps=num_warmup_steps,
344	            num_training_steps=num_training_steps,
345	            num_cycles=num_cycles,
346	            last_epoch=last_epoch,
347	        )
348	
349	    if name == SchedulerType.POLYNOMIAL:
350	        return schedule_func(
351	            optimizer,
352	            num_warmup_steps=num_warmup_steps,
353	            num_training_steps=num_training_steps,
354	            power=power,
355	            last_epoch=last_epoch,
356	        )
357	
358	    return schedule_func(
359	        optimizer, num_warmup_steps=num_warmup_steps, num_training_steps=num_training_steps, last_epoch=last_epoch
360	    )

306	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_edm_euler.EDMEulerScheduler._compute_karras_sigmas
307	    def _compute_karras_sigmas(
308	        self,
309	        ramp: torch.Tensor,
310	        sigma_min: float | None = None,
311	        sigma_max: float | None = None,
312	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
313	        """
314	        Construct the noise schedule of [Karras et al. (2022)](https://huggingface.co/papers/2206.00364).
315	
316	        Args:
317	            ramp (`torch.Tensor`):
318	                A tensor of values in [0, 1] representing the interpolation positions.
319	            sigma_min (`float`, *optional*):
320	                Minimum sigma value. If `None`, uses `self.config.sigma_min`.
321	            sigma_max (`float`, *optional*):
322	                Maximum sigma value. If `None`, uses `self.config.sigma_max`.
323	
324	        Returns:
325	            `torch.Tensor`:
326	                The computed Karras sigma schedule.
327	        """
328	        sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
329	        sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
330	
331	        rho = self.config.rho
332	        min_inv_rho = sigma_min ** (1 / rho)
333	        max_inv_rho = sigma_max ** (1 / rho)
334	        sigmas = (max_inv_rho + ramp * (min_inv_rho - max_inv_rho)) ** rho
335	        return sigmas

337	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_edm_euler.EDMEulerScheduler._compute_exponential_sigmas
338	    def _compute_exponential_sigmas(
339	        self,
340	        ramp: torch.Tensor,
341	        sigma_min: float | None = None,
342	        sigma_max: float | None = None,
343	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
344	        """
345	        Compute the exponential sigma schedule. Implementation closely follows k-diffusion:
346	        https://github.com/crowsonkb/k-diffusion/blob/6ab5146d4a5ef63901326489f31f1d8e7dd36b48/k_diffusion/sampling.py#L26
347	
348	        Args:
349	            ramp (`torch.Tensor`):
350	                A tensor of values representing the interpolation positions.
351	            sigma_min (`float`, *optional*):
352	                Minimum sigma value. If `None`, uses `self.config.sigma_min`.
353	            sigma_max (`float`, *optional*):
354	                Maximum sigma value. If `None`, uses `self.config.sigma_max`.
355	
356	        Returns:
357	            `torch.Tensor`:
358	                The computed exponential sigma schedule.
359	        """
360	        sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
361	        sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
362	        sigmas = torch.linspace(math.log(sigma_min), math.log(sigma_max), len(ramp)).exp().flip(0)
363	        return sigmas

365	    # Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_euler_discrete.EulerDiscreteScheduler._sigma_to_t
366	    def _sigma_to_t(self, sigma: np.ndarray, log_sigmas: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
367	        """
368	        Convert sigma values to corresponding timestep values through interpolation.
369	
370	        Args:
371	            sigma (`np.ndarray`):
372	                The sigma value(s) to convert to timestep(s).
373	            log_sigmas (`np.ndarray`):
374	                The logarithm of the sigma schedule used for interpolation.
375	
376	        Returns:
377	            `np.ndarray`:
378	                The interpolated timestep value(s) corresponding to the input sigma(s).
379	        """
380	        # get log sigma
381	        log_sigma = np.log(np.maximum(sigma, 1e-10))
382	
383	        # get distribution
384	        dists = log_sigma - log_sigmas[:, np.newaxis]
385	
386	        # get sigmas range
387	        low_idx = np.cumsum((dists >= 0), axis=0).argmax(axis=0).clip(max=log_sigmas.shape[0] - 2)
388	        high_idx = low_idx + 1
389	
390	        low = log_sigmas[low_idx]
391	        high = log_sigmas[high_idx]
392	
393	        # interpolate sigmas
394	        w = (low - log_sigma) / (low - high)
395	        w = np.clip(w, 0, 1)
396	
397	        # transform interpolation to time range
398	        t = (1 - w) * low_idx + w * high_idx
399	        t = t.reshape(sigma.shape)
400	        return t

412	                A tuple containing `alpha_t` (always 1 since inputs are pre-scaled) and `sigma_t` (same as input

420	    def convert_model_output(
421	        self,
422	        model_output: torch.Tensor,
423	        sample: torch.Tensor = None,
424	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
425	        """
426	        Convert the model output to the corresponding type the DPMSolver/DPMSolver++ algorithm needs. DPM-Solver is
427	        designed to discretize an integral of the noise prediction model, and DPM-Solver++ is designed to discretize an
428	        integral of the data prediction model.
429	
430	        > [!TIP] > The algorithm and model type are decoupled. You can use either DPMSolver or DPMSolver++ for both
431	        noise > prediction and data prediction models.
432	
433	        Args:
434	            model_output (`torch.Tensor`):
435	                The direct output from the learned diffusion model.
436	            sample (`torch.Tensor`):
437	                A current instance of a sample created by the diffusion process.
438	
439	        Returns:
440	            `torch.Tensor`:
441	                The converted model output.
442	        """
443	        sigma = self.sigmas[self.step_index]
444	        x0_pred = self.precondition_outputs(sample, model_output, sigma)
445	
446	        return x0_pred

448	    def dpm_solver_first_order_update(
449	        self,
450	        model_output: torch.Tensor,
451	        sample: torch.Tensor = None,
452	        noise: torch.Tensor | None = None,
453	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
454	        """
455	        One step for the first-order DPMSolver (equivalent to DDIM).
456	
457	        Args:
458	            model_output (`torch.Tensor`):
459	                The direct output from the learned diffusion model.
460	            sample (`torch.Tensor`):
461	                A current instance of a sample created by the diffusion process.
462	
463	        Returns:
464	            `torch.Tensor`:
465	                The sample tensor at the previous timestep.
466	        """
467	        sigma_t, sigma_s = (
468	            self.sigmas[self.step_index + 1],
469	            self.sigmas[self.step_index],
470	        )
471	        alpha_t, sigma_t = self._sigma_to_alpha_sigma_t(sigma_t)
472	        alpha_s, sigma_s = self._sigma_to_alpha_sigma_t(sigma_s)
473	        lambda_t = torch.log(alpha_t) - torch.log(sigma_t)
474	        lambda_s = torch.log(alpha_s) - torch.log(sigma_s)
475	
476	        h = lambda_t - lambda_s
477	        assert noise is not None
478	        x_t = (
479	            (sigma_t / sigma_s * torch.exp(-h)) * sample
480	            + (alpha_t * (1 - torch.exp(-2.0 * h))) * model_output
481	            + sigma_t * torch.sqrt(1.0 - torch.exp(-2 * h)) * noise
482	        )
483	
484	        return x_t

611	    def get_velocity(self, sample: torch.Tensor, noise: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.IntTensor) -> torch.Tensor:
612	        """
613	        Compute the velocity prediction from the sample and noise according to the velocity formula.
614	
615	        Args:
616	            sample (`torch.Tensor`):
617	                The input sample.
618	            noise (`torch.Tensor`):
619	                The noise tensor.
620	            timesteps (`torch.IntTensor`):
621	                The timesteps for velocity computation.
622	
623	        Returns:
624	            `torch.Tensor`:
625	                The computed velocity.
626	        """
627	        # Make sure alphas_cumprod and timestep have same device and dtype as sample
628	        self.alphas_cumprod = self.alphas_cumprod.to(device=sample.device)
629	        alphas_cumprod = self.alphas_cumprod.to(dtype=sample.dtype)
630	        timesteps = timesteps.to(sample.device)
631	
632	        sqrt_alpha_prod = alphas_cumprod[timesteps] ** 0.5
633	        sqrt_alpha_prod = sqrt_alpha_prod.flatten()
634	        while len(sqrt_alpha_prod.shape) < len(sample.shape):
635	            sqrt_alpha_prod = sqrt_alpha_prod.unsqueeze(-1)
636	
637	        sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = (1 - alphas_cumprod[timesteps]) ** 0.5
638	        sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod.flatten()
639	        while len(sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod.shape) < len(sample.shape):
640	            sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod.unsqueeze(-1)
641	
642	        velocity = sqrt_alpha_prod * noise - sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod * sample
643	        return velocity

648	    def previous_timestep(self, timestep: int) -> int | torch.Tensor:
649	        """
650	        Compute the previous timestep in the diffusion chain.
651	
652	        Args:
653	            timestep (`int`):
654	                The current timestep.
655	
656	        Returns:
657	            `int` or `torch.Tensor`:
658	                The previous timestep.
659	        """
660	        if self.custom_timesteps or self.num_inference_steps:
661	            index = (self.timesteps == timestep).nonzero(as_tuple=True)[0][0]
662	            if index == self.timesteps.shape[0] - 1:
663	                prev_t = torch.tensor(-1)
664	            else:
665	                prev_t = self.timesteps[index + 1]
666	        else:
667	            prev_t = timestep - 1
668	        return prev_t

48	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
49	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
50	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
51	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
52	) -> torch.Tensor:
53	    """
54	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
55	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
56	
57	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
58	    to that part of the diffusion process.
59	
60	    Args:
61	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
62	            The number of betas to produce.
63	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
64	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
65	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
66	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
67	
68	    Returns:
69	        `torch.Tensor`:
70	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
71	    """
72	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
73	
74	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
75	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
76	
77	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
78	
79	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
80	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
81	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
82	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
83	
84	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
85	
86	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
87	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
88	
89	    else:
90	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
91	
92	    betas = []
93	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
94	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
95	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
96	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
97	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
98

100	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr
101	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
102	    """
103	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
104	
105	    Args:
106	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
107	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
108	
109	    Returns:
110	        `torch.Tensor`:
111	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
112	    """
113	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
114	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
115	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
116	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
117	
118	    # Store old values.
119	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
120	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
121	
122	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
123	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
124	
125	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
126	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
127	
128	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
129	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
130	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
131	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
132	    betas = 1 - alphas
133	
134	    return betas

22	        assert self.scheduler.config.beta_end == 0.02

28	    def test_betas_are_cosine(self):
29	        """Betas should follow cosine annealing pattern."""

31	        # Cosine schedule: betas should decrease then increase (U-shape)
32	        # or follow the cosine annealing pattern

35	        assert betas[-1] >= 0

40	        for i in range(1, len(alphas_cumprod)):
41	            assert alphas_cumprod[i] <= alphas_cumprod[i - 1] + 1e-6

88	class ConfigMixin:
89	    r"""
90	    Base class for all configuration classes. All configuration parameters are stored under `self.config`. Also
91	    provides the [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] and [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] methods for loading, downloading, and
92	    saving classes that inherit from [`ConfigMixin`].
93	
94	    Class attributes:
95	        - **config_name** (`str`) -- A filename under which the config should stored when calling
96	          [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] (should be overridden by parent class).
97	        - **ignore_for_config** (`list[str]`) -- A list of attributes that should not be saved in the config (should be
98	          overridden by subclass).
99	        - **has_compatibles** (`bool`) -- Whether the class has compatible classes (should be overridden by subclass).
100	        - **_deprecated_kwargs** (`list[str]`) -- Keyword arguments that are deprecated. Note that the `init` function
101	          should only have a `kwargs` argument if at least one argument is deprecated (should be overridden by
102	          subclass).
103	    """
104	
105	    config_name = None
106	    ignore_for_config = []
107	    has_compatibles = False
108	
109	    _deprecated_kwargs = []
110	    _auto_class = None
111	
112	    @classmethod
113	    def register_for_auto_class(cls, auto_class="AutoModel"):
114	        """
115	        Register this class with the given auto class so that it can be loaded with `AutoModel.from_pretrained(...,
116	        trust_remote_code=True)`.
117	
118	        When the config is saved, the resulting `config.json` will include an `auto_map` entry mapping the auto class
119	        to this class's module and class name.
120	
121	        Args:
122	            auto_class (`str` or type, *optional*, defaults to `"AutoModel"`):
123	                The auto class to register this class with. Can be a string (e.g. `"AutoModel"`) or the class itself.
124	                Currently only `"AutoModel"` is supported.
125	
126	        Example:
127	
128	        ```python
129	        from diffusers import ModelMixin, ConfigMixin
130	
131	
132	        class MyCustomModel(ModelMixin, ConfigMixin): ...
133	
134	
135	        MyCustomModel.register_for_auto_class("AutoModel")
136	        ```
137	        """
138	        if auto_class != "AutoModel":
139	            raise ValueError(f"Only 'AutoModel' is supported, got '{auto_class}'.")
140	
141	        cls._auto_class = auto_class
142	
143	    def register_to_config(self, **kwargs):
144	        if self.config_name is None:
145	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Make sure that {self.__class__} has defined a class name `config_name`")
146	        # Special case for `kwargs` used in deprecation warning added to schedulers
147	        # TODO: remove this when we remove the deprecation warning, and the `kwargs` argument,
148	        # or solve in a more general way.
149	        kwargs.pop("kwargs", None)

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738	
739	
740	def flax_register_to_config(cls):
741	    original_init = cls.__init__
742	
743	    @functools.wraps(original_init)
744	    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
745	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
746	            raise RuntimeError(
747	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
748	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
749	            )
750	
751	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init. Retrieve all passed attributes
752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())
753	
754	        # Retrieve default values
755	        fields = dataclasses.fields(self)
756	        default_kwargs = {}
757	        for field in fields:
758	            # ignore flax specific attributes
759	            if field.name in self._flax_internal_args:
760	                continue
761	            if type(field.default) == dataclasses._MISSING_TYPE:
762	                default_kwargs[field.name] = None
763	            else:
764	                default_kwargs[field.name] = getattr(self, field.name)
765	
766	        # Make sure init_kwargs override default kwargs
767	        new_kwargs = {**default_kwargs, **init_kwargs}
768	        # dtype should be part of `init_kwargs`, but not `new_kwargs`
769	        if "dtype" in new_kwargs:
770	            new_kwargs.pop("dtype")
771	
772	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
773	        for i, arg in enumerate(args):
774	            name = fields[i].name
775	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
776	
777	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
778	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
779	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
780	
781	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
782	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
783	
784	    cls.__init__ = init
785	    return cls

74	class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
75	    """
76	    Base class for all schedulers.
77	
78	    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
79	    functionalities.
80	
81	    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
82	    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.
83	
84	    Class attributes:
85	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
86	          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
87	          by parent class).
88	    """
89	
90	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
91	    _compatibles = []
92	    has_compatibles = True
93

95	    @validate_hf_hub_args

97	        cls,
98	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
99	        subfolder: str | None = None,
100	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ) -> Self:
103	        r"""
104	        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.
105	
106	        Parameters:
107	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
108	                Can be either:
109	
110	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
111	                      the Hub.
112	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
114	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
115	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
116	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
118	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
119	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
120	                is not used.
121	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
122	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
123	                cached versions if they exist.
124	
125	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
126	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
128	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
129	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
130	            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
131	                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
132	                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
133	            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
134	                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
135	                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
136	            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
137	                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
138	                allowed by Git.
139	
140	        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
141	        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >

143	        firewalled environment.
144	
145	        """

147	            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
148	            subfolder=subfolder,
149	            return_unused_kwargs=True,
150	            return_commit_hash=True,
151	            **kwargs,
152	        )
153	        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive Report: Hugging Face Diffusers Scheduler Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the diffusers codebase, here's the complete analysis of how schedulers are registered, exported, and tested:

---

### 1. SCHEDULER REGISTRATION & EXPORTS AT TOP-LEVEL

#### Main Entry Point: `/src/diffusers/__init__.py`
- **Pattern Used**: Lazy module loading with `_import_structure` dictionary
- **Lines 36-46**: Main scheduler list initialization (empty list that gets extended)
- **Lines 341-384**: Core schedulers extended into `_import_structure["schedulers"]`
- **Lines 398, 411**: Optional schedulers added conditionally based on dependencies
  - `LMSDiscreteScheduler` (requires torch + scipy)
  - `CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler`, `DPMSolverSDEScheduler` (require torch + torchsde)

#### Scheduler Module: `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py`
**Full structure (lines 15-231):**
- **Import checks**: Conditional imports based on dependency availability (torch, flax, scipy, torchsde)
- **`_import_structure` and `_dummy_modules`** (lines 29-30): Lazy loading mechanism
- **PyTorch Schedulers** (lines 40-81): 40+ scheduler classes exported conditionally
- **Flax Schedulers** (lines 92-99): 8 JAX-based scheduler variants
- **Type Checking Block** (lines 131-224): Actual imports when `TYPE_CHECKING=True` or `DIFFUSERS_SLOW_IMPORT=True`
- **Lazy Module Setup** (lines 225-231): Uses `_LazyModule` for deferred imports

**All PyTorch Schedulers Exported:**
```
AmusedScheduler, CMStochasticIterativeScheduler, CogVideoXDDIMScheduler, CogVideoXDPMScheduler,
DDIMInverseScheduler, DDIMParallelScheduler, DDIMScheduler, DDPMParallelScheduler, DDPMScheduler,
DDPMWuerstchenScheduler, DEISMultistepScheduler, DPMSolverMultistepInverseScheduler,
DPMSolverMultistepScheduler, DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler, EDMDPMSolverMultistepScheduler,
EDMEulerScheduler, EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler,
FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler, FlowMatchHeunDiscreteScheduler, FlowMatchLCMScheduler,
HeliosDMDScheduler, HeliosScheduler, HeunDiscreteScheduler, IPNDMScheduler, KarrasVeScheduler,
KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler, KDPM2DiscreteScheduler, LCMScheduler, LTXEulerAncestralRFScheduler,
PNDMScheduler, RePaintScheduler, SASolverScheduler, SchedulerMixin, SCMScheduler, ScoreSdeVeScheduler,
TCDScheduler, UnCLIPScheduler, UniPCMultistepScheduler, VQDiffusionScheduler, LMSDiscreteScheduler,
CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler, DPMSolverSDEScheduler
```

---

### 2. BASE CLASSES & CONFIGURATION SYSTEM

#### `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`

**SchedulerMixin** (lines 74-177):
- Base class for ALL schedulers
- Inherits from `PushToHubMixin`
- **Key Methods**:
  - `from_pretrained()` (line 96): Load from HF Hub or local directory
  - `save_pretrained()` (line 155): Save configuration
  - `compatibles` property (line 172): Get compatible scheduler classes
- **Class Attributes**:
  - `config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME` (= "scheduler_config.json")
  - `_compatibles = []`: List of compatible scheduler class names
  - `has_compatibles = True`: Flag for compatibility system

**KarrasDiffusionSchedulers** (lines 33-49):
- Enum class mapping scheduler names to values

class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    """Base class for all schedulers"""

    has_compatibles = True

    def from_pretrained(
        cls,
        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
        subfolder: str | None = None,
        return_unused_kwargs=False,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> Self
    
    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs)
    
    @property
    def compatibles(self)
    
    @classmethod

    """Base class for configuration management"""

    ) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]

- Automatically captures all `__init__` arguments (except those starting with `_`)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775464521855-bvccus/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep.py`

**Unique Pattern - Sigma-based instead of Beta-based:**
```python
class CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    @register_to_config
    def __init__(
        self,
        sigma_min: float = 0.3,
        sigma_max: float = 500,
        sigma_data: float = 1.0,
        sigma_schedule: Literal["exponential", "karras"] = "exponential",  # Cosine variant uses sigma
        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
        solver_order: int = 2,
        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "v_prediction",
        rho: float = 7.0,
        solver_type: Literal["midpoint", "heun"] = "midpoint",
        lower_order_final: bool = True,
        euler_at_final: bool = False,
        final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
    )

def _compute_exponential_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Compute exponential sigma schedule"""
    sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
    sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
    sigmas = torch.linspace(math.log(sigma_min), math.log(sigma_max), len(ramp)).exp().flip(0)
    return sigmas

def _compute_karras_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Compute Karras sigma schedule from EDM paper"""
    sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
    sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
    rho = self.config.rho
    min_inv_rho = sigma_min ** (1 / rho)
    max_inv_rho = sigma_max ** (1 / rho)
    sigmas = (max_inv_rho + ramp * (min_inv_rho - max_inv_rho)) ** rho
    return sigmas
```

### 4. COSINE-BASED SCHEDULER ANALYSIS

**Cosine schedulers found (31 files mention "cosine"):**

1. **Primary cosine scheduler:** `scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep.py`
2. **Cosine beta function in all schedulers:** `betas_for_alpha_bar()` function with `alpha_transform_type="cosine"`

**Cosine Beta Schedule Implementation:**
```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Creates beta schedule from alpha_bar function"""
    
    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
    
    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
            snr = math.exp(lmb)
            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
    
    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
    
    betas = []
    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
```

**Key cosine feature:** Smooth, continuous noise schedule using cosine function with offset (0.008) and scaling (1.008)

### 5. BETA SCHEDULE TYPES

**Implemented beta_schedule options across schedulers:**

1. **"linear"** - Linear interpolation between beta_start and beta_end

2. **"scaled_linear"** - Scaled linear (used in Latent Diffusion Models)

    torch.linspace(beta_start**0.5, beta_end**0.5, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32) ** 2
)
```

3. **"squaredcos_cap_v2"** - Cosine-based (Glide cosine schedule)
```python

```

4. **"laplace"** - Laplace-based schedule
```python

```

5. **"sigmoid"** - Sigmoid-based schedule
```python

self.betas = torch.sigmoid(betas) * (beta_end - beta_start) + beta_start
```

6. **"trained_betas"** - Custom pre-computed betas passed directly

```

### 8. KEY PATTERNS AND CONVENTIONS

**Config Storage:**
- All parameters stored in `self._internal_dict` (FrozenDict)
- Accessed via `self.config.param_name` (not `self.param_name` directly - deprecated)
- Saved to JSON via `save_pretrained()`
- Loaded via `from_pretrained()` or `from_config()`

**Timestep Spacing Options:**
- `"linspace"` - Linear spacing in timestep space
- `"leading"` - Leading step ratio approach
- `"trailing"` - Trailing step ratio approach

**Prediction Types:**
- `"epsilon"` - Predicts noise
- `"sample"` - Predicts denoised sample directly
- `"v_prediction"` - Predicts velocity (EDM)

**Variance Types (DDPM-specific):**
- `"fixed_small"` - Fixed small variance
- `"fixed_small_log"` - Log of fixed small variance
- `"fixed_large"` - Fixed large variance
- `"fixed_large_log"` - Log of fixed large variance
- `"learned"` - Model predicts variance
- `"learned_range"` - Model predicts variance range

**Special Features:**
- `rescale_betas_zero_snr` - Rescales schedule to have zero terminal SNR
- `use_karras_sigmas` - Applies Karras sigma scaling (for sigma-based schedulers)
- `use_exponential_sigmas` - Exponential sigma scaling
- `use_beta_sigmas` - Beta-based sigma scaling

---

### 9. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**Alpha/Beta Schedule Relationships:**
```
alpha_t = 1 - beta_t
alpha_cumprod_t = ∏(alpha_i) for i in [0..t]
sigma_t = sqrt((1 - alpha_cumprod_t) / alpha_cumprod_t)

Forward process: x_t = sqrt(alpha_cumprod_t) * x_0 + sqrt(1 - alpha_cumprod_t) * noise
Reverse process: x_{t-1} = predicted_x_0 * coeff_0 + x_t * coeff_t + noise * variance
```

**Timestep Convention:**
- Timesteps typically descend: [999, 998, ..., 1, 0]
- In set_timesteps(), reversed to match inference order
- Custom timesteps must be in descending order

**Device/Dtype Handling:**
- Schedulers handle device placement dynamically
- alphas_cumprod cached on CPU by default (`.to("cpu")`)
- Moved to correct device in step() when needed
- Dtype conversion happens to match sample dtype

---

This comprehensive analysis provides the foundation for understanding how to implement new schedulers in the diffusers library!

#### **CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler (Cosine-based)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775464521855-bvccus/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep.py`

**Unique Pattern - Sigma-based instead of Beta-based:**
```python
class CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    @register_to_config
    def __init__(
        self,
        sigma_min: float = 0.3,
        sigma_max: float = 500,
        sigma_data: float = 1.0,
        sigma_schedule: Literal["exponential", "karras"] = "exponential",  # Cosine variant uses sigma
        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
        solver_order: int = 2,
        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "v_prediction",
        rho: float = 7.0,
        solver_type: Literal["midpoint", "heun"] = "midpoint",
        lower_order_final: bool = True,
        euler_at_final: bool = False,
        final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
    )

def _compute_exponential_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Compute exponential sigma schedule"""
    sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
    sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
    sigmas = torch.linspace(math.log(sigma_min), math.log(sigma_max), len(ramp)).exp().flip(0)
    return sigmas

def _compute_karras_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Compute Karras sigma schedule from EDM paper"""
    sigma_min = sigma_min or self.config.sigma_min
    sigma_max = sigma_max or self.config.sigma_max
    rho = self.config.rho
    min_inv_rho = sigma_min ** (1 / rho)
    max_inv_rho = sigma_max ** (1 / rho)
    sigmas = (max_inv_rho + ramp * (min_inv_rho - max_inv_rho)) ** rho
    return sigmas
```

### 4. COSINE-BASED SCHEDULER ANALYSIS

**Cosine schedulers found (31 files mention "cosine"):**

1. **Primary cosine scheduler:** `scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep.py`
2. **Cosine beta function in all schedulers:** `betas_for_alpha_bar()` function with `alpha_transform_type="cosine"`

**Cosine Beta Schedule Implementation:**
```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Creates beta schedule from alpha_bar function"""
    
    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
    
    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
            snr = math.exp(lmb)
            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
    
    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
    
    betas = []
    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
```

**Key cosine feature:** Smooth, continuous noise schedule using cosine function with offset (0.008) and scaling (1.008)

### 8. KEY PATTERNS AND CONVENTIONS

**Config Storage:**
- All parameters stored in `self._internal_dict` (FrozenDict)
- Accessed via `self.config.param_name` (not `self.param_name` directly - deprecated)
- Saved to JSON via `save_pretrained()`
- Loaded via `from_pretrained()` or `from_config()`

**Timestep Spacing Options:**
- `"linspace"` - Linear spacing in timestep space
- `"leading"` - Leading step ratio approach
- `"trailing"` - Trailing step ratio approach

**Prediction Types:**
- `"epsilon"` - Predicts noise
- `"sample"` - Predicts denoised sample directly
- `"v_prediction"` - Predicts velocity (EDM)

**Variance Types (DDPM-specific):**
- `"fixed_small"` - Fixed small variance
- `"fixed_small_log"` - Log of fixed small variance
- `"fixed_large"` - Fixed large variance
- `"fixed_large_log"` - Log of fixed large variance
- `"learned"` - Model predicts variance
- `"learned_range"` - Model predicts variance range

**Special Features:**
- `rescale_betas_zero_snr` - Rescales schedule to have zero terminal SNR
- `use_karras_sigmas` - Applies Karras sigma scaling (for sigma-based schedulers)
- `use_exponential_sigmas` - Exponential sigma scaling
- `use_beta_sigmas` - Beta-based sigma scaling

### 9. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**Alpha/Beta Schedule Relationships:**
```
alpha_t = 1 - beta_t
alpha_cumprod_t = ∏(alpha_i) for i in [0..t]
sigma_t = sqrt((1 - alpha_cumprod_t) / alpha_cumprod_t)

Forward process: x_t = sqrt(alpha_cumprod_t) * x_0 + sqrt(1 - alpha_cumprod_t) * noise
Reverse process: x_{t-1} = predicted_x_0 * coeff_0 + x_t * coeff_t + noise * variance
```

**Timestep Convention:**
- Timesteps typically descend: [999, 998, ..., 1, 0]
- In set_timesteps(), reversed to match inference order
- Custom timesteps must be in descending order

**Device/Dtype Handling:**
- Schedulers handle device placement dynamically
- alphas_cumprod cached on CPU by default (`.to("cpu")`)
- Moved to correct device in step() when needed
- Dtype conversion happens to match sample dtype

### 5. betas_for_alpha_bar Function - Full Implementation

**Location:** Both DDPM and DDIM files contain identical implementations.

```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function.
    
    Args:
        num_diffusion_timesteps: Number of betas to produce
        max_beta: Maximum beta to use (< 1.0 for numerical stability)
        alpha_transform_type: "cosine" (default), "exp", or "laplace"
    
    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: Betas used by the scheduler
    """
    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2

    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
            snr = math.exp(lmb)
            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))

    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.exp(t * -12.0)

    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")

    betas = []
    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
```

**Key Points:**
- The **cosine schedule** uses: `cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * π/2)²`
- The offset `0.008` prevents issues at `t=0`
- Returns cumulative product of `(1-beta)` via `alpha_bar_fn`
- Beta is computed as: `min(1 - alpha_bar(t2) / alpha_bar(t1), max_beta)`

### 6. Zero Terminal SNR Rescaling Function

```python
def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR (Signal-to-Noise Ratio).
    Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
    
    Args:
        betas: The betas to rescale
    
    Returns:
        Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR
    """
    # Convert to alphas_bar_sqrt
    alphas = 1.0 - betas
    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
    
    # Store old values for scaling
    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
    
    # Shift so last timestep is zero
    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
    
    # Scale first timestep back to old value
    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
    
    # Convert back to betas
    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2
    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]
    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
    betas = 1 - alphas
    
    return betas
```

def register_to_config(init):
    r"""
    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from ConfigMixin.
    All arguments are automatically sent to self.register_for_config.
    
    Warning: Private arguments (starting with _) are not sent to init!
    """
    
    @functools.wraps(init)
    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
        # Separate private and public kwargs
        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
        
        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
            raise RuntimeError(...)
        
        # Get parameters from function signature
        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
        new_kwargs = {}
        signature = inspect.signature(init)
        parameters = {
            name: p.default 
            for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) 
            if i > 0 and name not in ignore
        }
        
        # Map positional args to kwargs
        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
            new_kwargs[name] = arg
        
        # Add remaining kwargs with defaults
        new_kwargs.update({
            k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
            for k, default in parameters.items()
            if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
        })
        
        # Track parameters that used defaults
        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(...)
        
        # Merge and call register_to_config
        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
    
    return inner_init
```

### 10. Available Beta Schedules

The following beta schedules are implemented across DDPM/DDIM:

1. **`"linear"`** - Linear interpolation from `beta_start` to `beta_end`
2. **`"scaled_linear"`** - Square-rooted linear interpolation (specific to latent diffusion)
3. **`"squaredcos_cap_v2"`** - Glide cosine schedule (uses `betas_for_alpha_bar()`)
4. **`"sigmoid"`** - Sigmoid-based schedule
5. **`"laplace"`** - Laplace-based schedule (DDPM only, via `betas_for_alpha_bar()`)

### 5. betas_for_alpha_bar Function - Full Implementation

**Location:** Both DDPM and DDIM files contain identical implementations.

```python
def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function.
    
    Args:
        num_diffusion_timesteps: Number of betas to produce
        max_beta: Maximum beta to use (< 1.0 for numerical stability)
        alpha_transform_type: "cosine" (default), "exp", or "laplace"
    
    Returns:
        torch.Tensor: Betas used by the scheduler
    """
    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2

    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
            snr = math.exp(lmb)
            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))

    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.exp(t * -12.0)

    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")

    betas = []
    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
```

**Key Points:**
- The **cosine schedule** uses: `cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * π/2)²`
- The offset `0.008` prevents issues at `t=0`
- Returns cumulative product of `(1-beta)` via `alpha_bar_fn`
- Beta is computed as: `min(1 - alpha_bar(t2) / alpha_bar(t1), max_beta)`

```python
def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR (Signal-to-Noise Ratio).
    Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
    
    Args:
        betas: The betas to rescale
    
    Returns:
        Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR
    """
    # Convert to alphas_bar_sqrt
    alphas = 1.0 - betas
    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
    
    # Store old values for scaling
    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
    
    # Shift so last timestep is zero
    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
    
    # Scale first timestep back to old value
    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
    
    # Convert back to betas
    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2
    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]
    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
    betas = 1 - alphas
    
    return betas
```

    sigma_min: float = 0.3,
    sigma_max: float = 500,
    sigma_data: float = 1.0,
    sigma_schedule: Literal["exponential", "karras"] = "exponential",
    num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
    solver_order: int = 2,
    prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "v_prediction",
    rho: float = 7.0,
    solver_type: Literal["midpoint", "heun"] = "midpoint",
    lower_order_final: bool = True,
    euler_at_final: bool = False,
    final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
) -> None

def _compute_karras_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor

def _compute_exponential_sigmas(
    self,
    ramp: torch.Tensor,
    sigma_min: float | None = None,
    sigma_max: float | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor

def precondition_noise(self, sigma: float | torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor
    # Returns: atan(sigma) / pi * 2

def set_timesteps(self, num_inference_steps: int | None = None, device: str | torch.device | None = None) -> None

def step(
    self,
    model_output: torch.Tensor,
    timestep: int | torch.Tensor,
    sample: torch.Tensor,
    generator: torch.Generator | None = None,
    return_dict: bool = True,
) -> SchedulerOutput | tuple

def add_noise(
    self,
    original_samples: torch.Tensor,
    noise: torch.Tensor,
    timesteps: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor
```

---

### 9. Test File for Cosine Scheduler

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775464521855-bvccus/tests/test_cosine_scheduler.py`

This test file expects a `CosineAnnealingScheduler` class that does **not currently exist** in the codebase. The test expects:
- `set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)`
- `add_noise(samples, noise, timesteps)`
- `step(model_output, timestep, sample)`
- Properties: `betas`, `alphas_cumprod`, `config`
- Must return `SchedulerOutput` from `step()`

---

### 10. Available Beta Schedules

The following beta schedules are implemented across DDPM/DDIM:

1. **`"linear"`** - Linear interpolation from `beta_start` to `beta_end`
2. **`"scaled_linear"`** - Square-rooted linear interpolation (specific to latent diffusion)
3. **`"squaredcos_cap_v2"`** - Glide cosine schedule (uses `betas_for_alpha_bar()`)
4. **`"sigmoid"`** - Sigmoid-based schedule
5. **`"laplace"`** - Laplace-based schedule (DDPM only, via `betas_for_alpha_bar()`)

---

### 11. Summary: Key Components for Building a Cosine Annealing Scheduler

**Essential imports:**
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass
import math
import torch
import numpy as np
from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
from ..utils import BaseOutput
from ..utils.torch_utils import randn_tensor
from .scheduling_utils import SchedulerMixin, SchedulerOutput
```

**Essential base classes:**
- `SchedulerMixin` - Provides loading/saving, compatibility checks
- `ConfigMixin` - Provides config management (via `@register_to_config`)

**Essential utility functions:**
- `betas_for_alpha_bar()` - Already implements cosine schedules
- `rescale_zero_terminal_snr()` - For optional SNR rescaling
- `randn_tensor()` - For noise generation (handles device/dtype/generator)

**Essential data structures:**
- Use `@dataclass` and `BaseOutput` for outputs (like `DDPMSchedulerOutput`)
- Store `alphas_cumprod` as main state variable for noise scaling
- Maintain `timesteps` as torch tensor

**Key patterns observed:**
1. All schedulers inherit from both `SchedulerMixin` and `ConfigMixin`
2. Use `@register_to_config` decorator on `__init__` to auto-save config
3. `set_timesteps()` computes the schedule for inference
4. `step()` does one denoising step
5. `add_noise()` adds noise for training/forward diffusion
6. `__len__()` returns `num_train_timesteps`

---

All file paths are absolute and ready for reference. The codebase provides excellent utilities and patterns for building a cosine annealing scheduler!

30	@dataclass
31	class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
32	    """
33	    Output class for the scheduler's `step` function output.
34	
35	    Args:
36	        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
37	            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
38	            denoising loop.
39	        pred_original_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
40	            The predicted denoised sample `(x_{0})` based on the model output from the current timestep.
41	            `pred_original_sample` can be used to preview progress or for guidance.
42	    """
43	
44	    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
45	    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None

47	
48	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
49	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
50	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
51	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
52	) -> torch.Tensor:
53	    """
54	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
55	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
56	
57	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
58	    to that part of the diffusion process.
59	
60	    Args:
61	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
62	            The number of betas to produce.
63	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
64	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
65	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
66	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
67	
68	    Returns:
69	        `torch.Tensor`:
70	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
71	    """
72	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
73	
74	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
75	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
76	
77	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
78	
79	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
80	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
81	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
82	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
83	
84	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
85	
86	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
87	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
88	
89	    else:
90	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
91	
92	    betas = []
93	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
94	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
95	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
96	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
97	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
98	
99	
100	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr
101	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
102	    """
103	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
104	
105	    Args:
106	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
107	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
108	
109	    Returns:
110	        `torch.Tensor`:
111	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
112	    """
113	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
114	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
115	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
116	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
117	
118	    # Store old values.
119	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
120	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
121	
122	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
123	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
124	
125	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
126	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
127	
128	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
129	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
130	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
131	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
132	    betas = 1 - alphas
133	
134	    return betas
135
